title: Python application conventions
description: Typed Python runtime rules for explicit boundaries, safer dynamic access, and common debug hazards.

rules.title: Avoid getattr in normal application flow
rules.reason: Flag dynamic getattr calls so agents prefer explicit fields, typed adapters, or real boundary objects.

rules.title: Avoid broad object types
rules.reason: Flag broad object type usage so code moves toward concrete models, unions, Protocols, or TypedDict-style structures.

rules.title: Avoid committed print debugging
rules.reason: Flag print calls in Python application code where logging or structured diagnostics should be used.

rules.title: Avoid eval and exec
rules.reason: Flag eval and exec calls because they are rarely appropriate in normal application code.

rules.title: Avoid assert for runtime validation
rules.reason: Flag assert statements in runtime code because optimized Python execution can remove them.

title: Python PEP 8 Style
description: PEP 8-inspired rules for readable Python module style.

rules.title: Avoid wildcard imports
rules.reason: Flag wildcard imports so dependencies stay explicit and easy to trace.

rules.title: Avoid bare except clauses
rules.reason: Flag bare exception handlers so failure handling names the expected error.

rules.title: Use is None comparisons
rules.reason: Flag equality checks against None so code follows Python identity comparison conventions.

title: Python Typing Boundaries
description: Rules for typed Python APIs and fewer static-analysis escape hatches.

rules.title: Avoid broad type ignore comments
rules.reason: Flag broad type ignore comments so projects prefer narrow type fixes or documented exceptions.

rules.title: Avoid untyped public functions
rules.reason: Flag function boundaries that do not expose parameter or return types.

rules.title: Avoid importing Any as a default escape hatch
rules.reason: Flag Any imports so code moves toward concrete models, protocols, or unions.

title: Python Async Services
description: Rules for async Python services where blocking calls and hidden event loops hurt reliability.

rules.title: Avoid time.sleep in async-capable code
rules.reason: Flag blocking sleeps so async services use cancellable scheduling primitives.

rules.title: Avoid asyncio.run inside library flow
rules.reason: Flag hidden event-loop creation inside reusable code.

rules.title: Avoid untracked create_task calls
rules.reason: Flag task creation where cancellation and error ownership are not visible.

title: Go service conventions
description: Small Go rules for production service hygiene: context flow, panics, and debug output.

rules.title: Avoid context.TODO in application flow
rules.reason: Flag context.TODO calls so code accepts or derives an explicit lifecycle context.

rules.title: Avoid panic in normal service flow
rules.reason: Flag panic calls in service code where errors should usually be returned or handled.

rules.title: Avoid fmt print debugging
rules.reason: Flag fmt.Print-style calls that should usually become structured logging.

rules.title: Avoid process exits in service flow
rules.reason: Flag log.Fatal and os.Exit calls so reusable code returns errors to the application boundary.

title: Effective Go Inspired
description: Rules inspired by Effective Go: explicit errors, simple APIs, and readable package behavior.

rules.title: Avoid empty interface API boundaries
rules.reason: Flag broad interface{} contracts so APIs stay narrow and typed.

rules.title: Review init functions
rules.reason: Flag hidden package initialization so setup can become explicit.

rules.title: Avoid nil nil returns
rules.reason: Flag value-plus-error returns that provide neither data nor an explanation.

title: Go Concurrency Hygiene
description: Rules for goroutine lifecycle, request context flow, and cancellable service code.

rules.title: Avoid context.Background in request flow
rules.reason: Flag fresh background contexts so cancellation and tracing keep flowing.

rules.title: Review untracked goroutines
rules.reason: Flag goroutine launches that should be tied to a lifecycle owner.

rules.title: Review time.After allocations
rules.reason: Flag time.After use where reusable timers may be safer in hot paths.

title: Rust Application Conventions
description: Rules for Rust application code hygiene: explicit errors, no committed scaffolding, and fewer panic paths.

rules.title: Avoid committed dbg macros
rules.reason: Flag dbg! calls so debug probes do not land in application code.

rules.title: Review unwrap and expect calls
rules.reason: Flag Option and Result panic paths so code moves toward explicit error handling.

rules.title: Avoid panic in normal application flow
rules.reason: Flag panic! calls where returning errors or handling invalid states is usually clearer.

rules.title: Avoid committed todo and unimplemented macros
rules.reason: Flag scaffolding macros before unfinished branches are committed.

title: TypeScript application conventions
description: TypeScript rules for safer application code: no committed console debugging, no var, and fewer untyped escape hatches.

rules.title: Avoid committed console.log
rules.reason: Flag console.log calls in application code where logging, telemetry, or UI state should be explicit.

rules.title: Avoid var declarations
rules.reason: Flag var declarations so code uses block-scoped let or const.

rules.title: Avoid explicit any
rules.reason: Flag explicit any annotations so code moves toward unknown, generics, discriminated unions, or domain types.

rules.title: Avoid committed debugger statements
rules.reason: Flag debugger statements before they land in application code.

title: TypeScript React
description: Rules for React codebases using TypeScript and predictable component lifecycles.

rules.title: Avoid array index keys
rules.reason: Flag React index keys so lists use stable domain identifiers.

rules.title: Avoid async useEffect callbacks
rules.reason: Flag async effect callbacks so cancellation stays explicit.

rules.title: Review dangerous HTML rendering
rules.reason: Flag direct HTML injection so sanitization and ownership boundaries are reviewed.
